Hickam_Orientation: The human input module and the AI topological structure module are conceptually distinct bounded contexts. If they share a global state tree or mutate the same data entities locally, it triggers a Semantic Saponification and a Shared Database Anti-Pattern (SCAR-002), collapsing the Mereological boundaries.
Contrastive_Delta: Instead of prompting the AI for desired outcomes (which collapses into high-entropy semantic generalizations), the strategy uses Failure-Informed Prompt Inversion (FIPI). The human defines the failures (Scars) and the AI routes its generated structure *around* them. To support this technically, the UI architecture is moving from a monolithic implicit state model to an event-driven isolation model.
